8 bit.gadget: Cheat Cartridge most of the time, including Heist. Extra Credits is usable in Heist but less flexible.
8 bit.starpower: Boosted Booster most of the time. Plugged In only in nearly unwinnable long-range matchups.

alli.gear: Speed and Damage or Damage and Shield.

amber.gadget: Dancing Flames overall. Fire Starters is seeing more use on open maps.
amber.starpower: Wild Flames overall, though Scorching Siphon is also used by some pros.

angelo.gadget: Stinging Flight more often in recent Knockout/Bounty metas with Mortis, Alli, and Kit pressure. Master Fletcher is still matchup dependent.
angelo.gear: Damage and Shield. Health only if you struggle with Angelo.
angelo.tips: Can hold down autoaim to charge an auto aimed shot.

ash.gadget: Chill Pill. Rotten Banana can be experimented with after its health-loss change.
ash.hypercharge: Rats overwhelm opponents and allow cycling through multiple supers, turning him into a wipe machine.

barley.gadget: Sticky Syrup Mixer on ladder. Herbal Tonic can be strong in Hot Zone.
barley.starpower: Extra Noxious. Medical Use into another thrower.

bea.gadget: Rattled Hive overall. Honey Molasses can be used in Knockout/Bounty to tank Gene pull or sniper pressure.
bea.tips: Slow projectile speed mean bea is best at midrange rather than max range.

belle.gadget: Nest Egg. Reverse Polarity is only for fun or niche plays.
belle.starpower: Positive Feedback. Grounded only into Mortis.

berry.gadget: Friendship Is Great everywhere except Heist. Healthy Additives in Heist.
berry.gear: Damage and Shield. Gadget Cooldown is also usable.
berry.tips: Attack is impact + damage zone after. Damage zone doesn't stack. Weak vs tanks

bo.gadget: Super Totem most of the time, even in Heist. Tripwire can be used on ladder or some Brawl Ball/Heist spots.
bo.hypercharge: Wide-area damage that guarantees kills and shreds Heist safes, with fast cycling via mines.

bonnie.hypercharge: Insanely strong because she can land multiple stuns per hypercharge and kill even the tankiest brawlers.

brock.gadget: Rocket Fuel most of the time to open the map. Rocket Laces can be better in rare pressure matchups.
brock.tips: User super and then hypercharge for max dmg.

bull.gadget: T-Bone Injector in competitive and hard matchups. Stomper can be better in easier matchups to confirm kills.
bull.starpower: Tough Guy. Berserker can work, but Bull is usually too squishy without Tough Guy.
bull.gear: Damage and Shield. Shield is preferred over Speed/Health even on bushy maps for stronger one-vs-one trades.

buster.gear: Speed and Damage on open maps. Health can replace Speed on less open maps.

buzz.gadget: Reserve Buoy overall because it pairs well with hypercharge. X-Ray-Shades only on very bushy maps.
buzz.starpower: Eyes Sharp overall. Tougher Torpedo when using Buzz to counter tanks and assassins.
buzz.hypercharge: Becomes “Spider-Man,” zipping across the map with unlimited stuns to make game-changing plays.

byron.gadget: Booster Shots most of the time. Shot in the Arm only if your whole job is supporting teammates.
byron.starpower: Malaise. Injection into pets or Ring of Fire.
byron.hypercharge: Extends his versatility by giving a bigger AoE super that charges fast and can be used aggressively.
byron.tips: Autoaiming super almost always works perfectly.

carl.gadget: Heat Ejector most of the time. Flying Hook in Bounty and Heist to get behind enemy lines and create pressure.
carl.tips: Before supering, throw out one attack.

charlie.gadget: Spiders most of the time. Personal Space can be underrated in Gem Grab or when enemies pierce spiders.
charlie.gear: Gadget Cooldown into snipers. Damage or Health are also usable.

chester.gear: Damage and Shield. Vision can be good on very bushy maps.

chuck.gadget: Rerouting on straight-line Heist maps like Safe Zone. Ghost Train on some other Heist maps or outside Heist.
chuck.tips: Putting post above safe lets you hit safe twice on the way back.

clancy.gadget: Tactical Retreat, especially after the cooldown buff. Snappy Shooting is still usable.
clancy.starpower: Recon. Avoid Pumping Up except into triple tank/assassin comps.
clancy.tips: Stage one sucks and requires getting out of asap.

colette.starpower: Push It in competitive for faster chains and knockback kills. Mass Tax is still good.
colette.gear: Gadget Cooldown most of the time. Vision can be good on bushy/open maps.

colt.gadget: Speedloader most of the time. Silver Bullet only when wall break is required and other wall breakers are banned.
colt.gear: Damage and Shield. Gadget Cooldown is also strong if you want to spam Speedloader.

cordelius.gadget: Replanting most of the time. Poison Mushroom only when shutting down tanks or Mortis.
cordelius.starpower: Comboshrooms. Mushroom Kingdom can be good in bad matchups.

crow.gadget: Defense Booster overall. Slowing Toxin into Edgar, Mortis, or multiple aggressive brawlers.
crow.gear: Damage and Shield. Speed can be used but is often overkill.

darryl.gadget: Recoiling Rotator in Heist. Tar Barrel elsewhere.
darryl.tips: Tip of his super will knockback again.

doug.gadget: Extra Mustard most of the time. Double Sausage only in Knockout or heal cheese comps.
doug.gear: Damage and Shield/Speed depending on openness.
doug.hypercharge: Enables cheesy combos by hypercharging allies like Janet or Gray for unstoppable setups in Knockout/Bounty.

draco.starpower: Shredding outside Heist. Expose in Heist.
draco.hypercharge: Hard to charge but nearly guarantees team wipe.
draco.tips: Pairs great with Kit, Byron when supering. Great for when pressure is needed (hot zone, etc.)

dynamike.gadget: Fidget Spinner is often preferred by pros for survivability and chip into assassins. Satchel Charge is usable but has a long cooldown, so pair it with Gadget Cooldown.

edgar.starpower: Fisticuffs. Hard Landing is usable, but Fisticuffs is preferred for chaining kills.

el primo.starpower: El Fuego overall. Meteor Rush can be used in Brawl Ball.
el primo.gear: Damage or Super Charge, plus Shield.
el primo.tips: Super can be chained into suplex for a lot of CC.

emz.gadget: Friendzoner into aggro. Acid Spray is strong in ranked when assassins are not a threat.
emz.gear: Damage and Shield on closed maps. Speed can replace one gear on bushy maps.

eve.gear: Reload and Shield. Mythic gear is not worth it.

fang.gadget: Roundhouse Kick for combos. Corn-Fu is also strong for pressure/zoning into certain matchups.
fang.starpower: Divine Soles most of the time. Fresh Kicks only when it will realistically reset into multiple targets.

frank.starpower: Sponge. Power Grab has occasional use.
frank.gear: Health or Shield, with Speed/Damage depending on map.
frank.tips: Can wallpeek surprisingly well due to triangular attack.

gale.starpower: Blustery Blow when using Gale as tank/assassin shutdown. Freezing Snow on very open maps.
gale.gear: Damage and Shield. Vision can be strong on super bushy maps.

gene.gadget: Vengeful Spirits in Bounty/Knockout most of the time. Lamp Blowout into Mortis or aggro.
gene.gear: Talk to the Hand plus Shield on open maps like Shooting Star/Belle's Rock, or Vision on grassy maps.
gene.tips: Move backwards after hitting a pull for longer CC.

gray.starpower: New Perspective in Gem Grab/Hot Zone. Fake Injury in Bounty/Knockout.
gray.tips: Can super backwards after hitting pull gadget to extend pull distance.

griff.starpower: Keep the Change for extra DPS. Business Resilience only in very bad matchups to be annoying.
griff.gear: Gadget Cooldown to open maps. Damage or Health are also usable.
griff.hypercharge: Chaining supers through walls lets him counter tanks and pull off devastating wipes.
griff.tips: Midrange wallbreaker. Use on any map you need a wallbreak + midrange on

gus.gear: Damage and Shield. Health is better in Bounty or into snipers.
gus.hypercharge: His hypercharge super melts any assassin/tank that approaches, easy to cycle thanks to accurate shots.
gus.tips: Super niche but can spawn 2 ghosts if you shoot quickly and hit at max range.

hank.gadget: Barricade overall. Water Balloons is strong into Edgar, Mortis, and tanks.
hank.starpower: It's Gonna Blow. Take Cover only on extremely bushy maps, often with Water Balloons.
hank.gear: Health and Damage. Shield is also usable.
hank.hypercharge: Cycles hypercharge quickly and loves the speed/damage buffs, suffocating opponents with torpedoes.

jacky.starpower: Counter Crush. Hardy Hard Hat into heavy sniper/range comps.

jae yong.gadget: Weekend Warrior for extra damage and wall pressure. Slow Song is okay but less preferred.
jae yong.tips: Really versatile in many team comps.

janet.gadget: Backstage Pass in Gem Grab and into assassins/aggro. Drop the Bass on bushy maps.
janet.gear: Vision and Shield on bushy Gem Grab maps.
janet.hypercharge: One of the best hypercharges in the game, buy it.

jessie.gadget: Spark Plug. Recoil Spring only in lower ranks or Heist cheese.

juju.gadget: Elementalist most of the time. Voodoo Chile is also usable for the slow.
juju.starpower: Guarded Gris-Gris most of the time to waste ammo. Numbing Needles into assassins.

kaze.gadget: Gracious Host is preferred in pro play. Hensojutsu can work against weaker players.
kaze.hypercharge: In ninja form, dashes through weakened enemies for instant kills and easy super cycles.

kenji.hypercharge: Still really good post nerfs, but no longer guaranteed team wipes.
kenji.tips: Can dash into wall and then use swipe to wall peak for more dmg/range

kit.starpower: Overly Attached in 3v3. Power Hungry in Showdown.
kit.hypercharge: Turns him into an assassin by letting him chain hypercharge supers for back-to-back kills.

larry & lawrie.gear: Damage and Shield. Vision or Gadget Cooldown are optional.

leon.gear: Damage and Shield. Gadget Cooldown is optional for more Lollipop Drop spam.

lily.gear: Damage and Shield. Gadget Cooldown can be better in very tough matchups to spam Vanish.
lily.hypercharge: Teleports enemies into the “shadow realm” where they’re helpless, and charges quickly for repeated kills.

lola.gadget: Freeze Frame overall. Stunt Double can heal both Lola and her ego and is situationally good.
lola.gear: Reload plus Damage/Vision depending on Heist or bushy maps.

lou.gadget: Cryo Syrup most of the time. Ice Block is often used by pros in Hot Zone.
lou.hypercharge: Instantly freezes enemies with no counterplay, then cycles into another super for cleanup.

lumi.tips: Positioning attacks for return is very important.

maisie.gear: Damage and Shield. Health can be underrated.

mandy.gadget: Cookie Crumbs most of the time to build super. Caramelize can be better on super open maps.
mandy.gear: Health and Shield. Damage is also useful with her super.
mandy.hypercharge: Boosts her damage enough to one-shot shielded snipers and meta brawlers, great for clutch team wipes.
mandy.tips: Try moving a little bit after every attack instead of just standing still.

max.starpower: Run N Gun. Super Charged only when Max is mainly enabling outranged teammates.
max.hypercharge: Gives her whole team speed plus ¾ of a super, enabling massive tempo swings in fights.

meeple.gadget: Both gadgets are strong and worth owning; choose based on matchup.
meeple.starpower: Do Not Pass Go unless the map is very open. Rule Bending is better on very open maps.
meeple.tips: Best matchups are vs midhp brawlers (~6k range). Always preserve ammo to win engagement due to slow reload. Use super offensively on enemy walls.

mico.gadget: Clipping Scream most of the time. Presto on a few maps.
mico.starpower: Monkey Business outside Heist. Record Smash in Heist.

mina.gadget: Windmill overall. Capo-What? into Mortis or tanks.
mina.gear: Damage and Shield. Health can be good too.
mina.tips: good in gem grab, brawl ball

moe.starpower: Skipping Stones most of the time. Speeding Ticket into control comps where charging super is hard.
moe.gear: Damage and Shield/Vision on bushy maps.

mortis.gadget: Both gadgets are strong. Combo Spinner is usually safer, while Creature of the Night rewards skilled timing.
mortis.gear: Damage and Shield for safer play. Aggressive Mortis players can use Speed or Health with Damage.
mortis.hypercharge: Can chain bat supers through multiple enemies without getting close, leading massive pushes.

mr p.gadget: Porter Reinforcements overall to waste ammo. Service Bell only if you know you can get super.
mr p.hypercharge: Forces enemies to waste shots on hypercharged ports, shutting down single-shot brawlers like Piper or Jean.

nani.gadget: Return to Sender overall, especially into snipers. Warp Blast in Heist, Brawl Ball, or niche modes.
nani.gear: Super Charge or Health, with Shield as needed.
nani.tips: Overshoot behind a little to more reliably hit 2 shots

nita.gadget: Faux Fur. Bear Paws can work in some matchups, but Faux Fur is safer and strong into tanks like Bull.
nita.starpower: Bear With Me. Hyper Bear in Heist.

ollie.gadget: All Eyez on Me most of the time. Regulate on very open maps or very tough matchups.
ollie.starpower: Renegade most of the time. Kick, Push can be good on Belle's Rock.
ollie.hypercharge: Hypercharge lets him wipe teams with huge damage output, though it takes over three supers to charge.
ollie.tips: After hypnotizing, if you have teammate nearby, walk in straight line to make enemy easy to hit. Can auto-aim to shorten super distance.

otis.gear: Vision on bushy maps, otherwise Damage and Shield. Super Charge is not very effective.

pam.starpower: Mama's Squeeze. Mama's Hug can be better on very open Hot Zone maps.
pam.gear: Health or Gadget Cooldown, with Super Turret/Vision depending on map.

pearl.gear: Shield plus Health/Damage/Vision depending on map.

penny.starpower: Heavy Coffers most of the time. Master Blaster into Mortis or other assassins.
penny.gear: Damage and Shield. Health can be good in Hot Zone.

piper.gadget: Homemade Recipe in Bounty/Knockout and long-range modes. Auto Aimer can be better on ladder or into aggression.
piper.gear: Health and Shield in Bounty/Knockout sniper matchups. Damage is optional elsewhere.
piper.hypercharge: Adds damage and terrain opening to her jump, enabling aggressive plays and two-taps.

poco.gadget: Tuning Fork overall. Protective Tunes in ranked to counter CC like Lou.

r t.gadget: Out of Line almost always. Hacks! only on very open Bounty/Knockout maps.

rico.gadget: Multiball Launcher most of the time. Bouncy Castle is improved and can be better in rough matchups.
rico.starpower: Super Bouncy overall. Robo Retreat into very bad counters.
rico.gear: Reload or Vision, plus Shield/Damage depending on map.
rico.hypercharge: Unlimited bounce hypercharge cycles supers on the right maps, making him toxic and unstoppable.

rosa.gadget: Grow Light in competitive for grass cheese and map control.
rosa.starpower: Plant Life on Knockout/Bounty grass setups. Thorny Gloves in Brawl Ball or more aggressive modes.

ruffs.gadget: Take Cover is stronger into Pierce/Meeple and single-shot threats. Air Support otherwise.
ruffs.starpower: Air Superiority. Field Promotion in Knockout.
ruffs.gear: Vision or Damage. Gadget Cooldown is good when spamming Take Cover into Pierce/Meeple.

sandy.tips: Try to use super in a way that enables you to charge your super back. (Generally avoid using unless you have full hp and can push up with team).

shade.tips:
Heavily enabled by walls. Great in brawl ball, gem grab, and hot zone.
Matchup list according to spen: https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/931255199627112458/1314104641247514624/The_Only_Shade_Guide_Youll_Ever_Need_4-11_screenshot.png?ex=67528ed1&is=67513d51&hm=50f1dee469c3079dcde589f9abda83b33c46a33bfd4f2dceffb288ba4fa597f4&

shelly.gear: Speed and Damage. Shield can also be used depending on map.

spike.gadget: Life Plant most of the time. Popping Pincushion into Mortis, assassins, tanks, or Heist.
spike.gear: Damage or Vision depending on map and matchup, plus Shield.

squeak.gadget: Windup in Knockout/Bounty. Residue is still good in other modes.
squeak.starpower: Super Sticky most of the time. Chain Reaction can be solid with Windup.

stu.gadget: Breakthrough most of the time, especially to counter Rico or key walls. Speed Zone in Hot Zone or Gem Grab support setups.

surge.gadget: Power Surge in competitive to escape level one. Power Shield into tanks or assassins.
surge.gear: Damage and Shield. Speed can be used on bushy maps.

tara.gadget: Support From Beyond overall. Psychic Enhancer into Sandy, Penny, or multiple single-shot brawlers.
tara.starpower: Healing Shade overall. Black Portal can be better into multiple single-shot brawlers.
tara.gear: Shield/Health plus Speed/Vision depending on map.
tara.hypercharge: Charges in only two supers, nearly unmissable, breaks terrain, and makes her dominant in Gem Grab.

tick.gear: Thicc Head and Shield. Vision can work occasionally.

trunk.gear: Damage and Shield. Speed is less necessary because his main attack can boost movement.
trunk.hypercharge: Should be strong once bug is fixed, letting him instantly delete enemies when dashing through.

ziggy.tips: His super is why he is good, so don't waste it and try to save ammo so you can cycle supers.

gigi.gadget: Helping Hand is preferred, though Longer Strings is also usable after buffs.

pierce.gadget: Bubble Trouble into Edgar, Mortis, or assassins. Bottomless Mags otherwise.

glowbert.gadget: Spectral Surge is preferred because it helps charge super quickly when used on yourself and teammates. More Lumens is easier but weaker.
glowbert.tips: Best in any control map, especially on more open maps. Reconnect beam to burst an extra 300 dmg. Glowbert is all about good positioning.

sirius.tips: Try to keep your shadows alive and sync pushes with team. Will dominate control modes

najia.gadget: Gem Grab gadget in Gem Grab. Poison puddles/other gadget in other modes.
najia.starpower: Most pros use the first star power. The other can be better into sharpshooters, single-shot brawlers, or aggressive modes where you get more kills.
najia.gear: Damage or Gadget Cooldown, plus Shield/utility depending on mode.

damian.gadget: Spiritual Healing most of the time. The range/cheese gadget has niche use into many ranged brawlers.
